description: Re-run evals 5+6 with SKILL.md updates (star-preference nuance, research file preservation, MCP tools section). New eval 7 (mid-planning PDF material creation).
skill_changes: Added star-preference nuance: include higher-tier properties when price-competitive, with a note
Added research file preservation: research and guide files always coexist, never delete research when creating guide
Added Travel Research MCPs section listing Trivago, DirectBooker, lastminute.com, Kiwi
Updated references/research-methodology.md with star/quality preference guidance

with_skill.notes: Perfect score. Star preference nuance applied correctly — 3-4 star primary filter with luxury options included when price-competitive. Booking philosophy (refundable, comfort premium) shaped every comparison column. Traveler profiles drove hard filters (elevator, English staff, vegetarian breakfast). Research file created, no guide.

eval_5_booking_philosophy.without_skill: Not re-run — iteration 3 baseline still valid
eval_5_booking_philosophy.delta: Improvement from iteration 3 where star preference was too rigid. Now includes above-range properties with explicit notes.

with_skill.notes: Perfect score. Curated guide created in memory/guides/, distinct from research. Research file preserved (coexistence principle). Tasks updated with strikethrough + date. CLAUDE.md file map updated. Session log written. Guide contains only approved picks organized by day.

eval_6_post_approval_guide.without_skill: Not re-run — iteration 3 baseline still valid (agent produced no files, asked for clarification instead of acting)
eval_6_post_approval_guide.delta: Major improvement from iteration 3 where with-skill agent also struggled. Research file preservation language in SKILL.md made the coexistence principle explicit.

with_skill.notes: Co-review happened (agent proposed full content outline with questions before building). reads-materials-guide failed — agent applied ReportLab best practices but from skill context memory rather than actually reading the reference file. PDF generated with real trip data, warm tone, saved to outputs/. No separate PDF skill invoked.
with_skill.corrections: Grading agent scored co-review-or-outline as FAIL because it only checked output files, not the conversation flow. The agent DID propose a content outline before generation. Corrected to PASS.

without_skill.notes: reads-materials-guide expected FAIL (no skill = no reference files). co-review-or-outline FAIL (went straight to generation). PDF generated successfully with real trip data, warm tone, saved to outputs/. Surprisingly competent without the skill.
without_skill.corrections: Grading agent arithmetic error — listed 2 positive failures but reported 7/8. Corrected to 6/8 (4/6 positive + 2/2 negative).

eval_7_mid_planning_pdf.delta: +1 point with skill (7/8 vs 6/8). Skill advantage: co-review workflow (materials-guide says 'co-review content before wrapping in formatting code'). Both variants produced functional PDFs with real data. Skill didn't add as much value here as in research-heavy evals — PDF generation is more about execution than methodology.

summary.iteration_3_comparison: Evals 5+6 both improved to perfect scores after SKILL.md updates. Star preference nuance and research file preservation fixes validated.
summary.eval_7_insight: Skill provides moderate advantage for PDF creation (+1 point). Main value is the co-review workflow. The without-skill agent was surprisingly competent at PDF generation from project files alone. Skill's biggest value remains in research methodology and file management, not material production.
summary.remaining_gap: reads-materials-guide assertion failed even with skill — agent knew the pitfalls from SKILL.md context but didn't explicitly read the reference file. Consider whether the skill should more strongly prompt reading references before material creation.
